gate_rows.name: shared core is authority state only
required_preconditions: shared core operations are freeze, bind, revoke_check, validate, settle, and release
shared core carries only CapSched authority state
shared core excludes Linux subsystem lifecycle mechanics
forbidden_fallbacks: shared core as generic Linux async subsystem
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch identifies shared-core fields and keeps subsystem mechanics in adapters

gate_rows.name: adapter mechanics stay separate
required_preconditions: workqueue adapter obligations are tracked separately
io_uring adapter obligations are tracked separately
one adapter lifecycle cannot prove the other
forbidden_fallbacks: workqueue coalescing model used as io_uring proof
io_uring request model used as workqueue proof
single adapter proof used for both surfaces
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch states which adapter surface it touches and cites that surface's obligations

gate_rows.name: workqueue refinement is required
required_preconditions: freeze before publication
queue_work false preserves first carrier
second candidate is rejected, settled, and released or explicitly modeled
delayed retime and self-requeue do not refresh receipts
worker and rescuer identity are not authority
cancel, flush, and pending clear are not monitor revoke receipts
caller BudgetTicket settlement is explicit
release is separate from Linux work lifetime
forbidden_fallbacks: cancel or flush as revoke proof
gate_rows.patch_precondition: workqueue-touching patch includes the N-127 obligation table

gate_rows.name: io_uring refinement is required
required_preconditions: SQE is consumed before freeze
resource authority is bound separately from io_rsrc_node liveness
resource generation snapshot is preserved
inline issue and io-wq worker issue validate before side effects
REQ_F_REISSUE does not refresh receipts
CQE is not settlement proof
cancel is not monitor revoke receipt
linked requests do not inherit authority implicitly
resource updates do not mutate in-flight authority
uring_cmd requires typed endpoint authority
gate_rows.patch_precondition: io_uring-touching patch includes the N-128 obligation table

gate_rows.name: effective authority is intersection
required_preconditions: caller frozen authority is present
service or resource authority is bound separately
effective authority is subset of both inputs before side effects
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch names authority intersection point before endpoint side effects

gate_rows.name: budget and settlement are caller scoped
required_preconditions: caller BudgetTicket or split child ticket exists before async publication
charge, refund, cancel, revoke, completion, retry, and rejected candidate settlement are explicit
settlement is at most once
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch includes BudgetTicket derivation and settlement table

gate_rows.name: revoke and freshness are not Linux cleanup
required_preconditions: epoch, generation, and revoke state are checked before side effects
cancel, flush, pending clear, completion, CQE, ref drop, and free are lifecycle events only
stale carriers are rejected before endpoint side effects
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch includes revoke/freshness ordering for queued, executing, cancel, retry, completion, and free

gate_rows.name: source anchors and drift rules are required
required_preconditions: candidate patch names touched Linux source anchors
source-only and semantic-validation flags remain explicit
traceability overlay preserves authority and protection non-claims
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch includes drift-aware source-anchor update or explicit blocker

gate_rows.name: evidence classes remain separate
required_preconditions: source maps, TLC models, build checks, traces, monitor tests, and production evidence remain separate claim classes
non-claims remain explicit in patch proposal
forbidden_fallbacks: JSON gate pass as implementation approval
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch records evidence class and non-claims

gate_rows.name: candidate patch scope is narrow
required_preconditions: candidate proposal states whether it touches shared core, workqueue adapter, io_uring adapter, or traceability only
behavior change remains blocked unless adapter-specific obligations are met
no production claim is attached to Linux-only prototype work
forbidden_fallbacks: behavior-changing enforcement from combined gate alone
security boundary claim from Linux-only model
gate_rows.patch_precondition: candidate patch may be drafted, but not approved, only after this gate is checked
